title: Interpret Load And Unload Direction Fields Between Cells
summary: Use the map or cell information view to interpret load and unload direction settings between cells as tote-dependent AGV travel permissions. The source explains that unload direction only allows AGVs without a tote, loaded direction only allows AGVs with a tote, and in most areas both are enabled. The source also states these fields are generally design-oriented and not a primary support control.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing a map or cell information view and you need to understand what displayed load or unload direction fields mean for AGV travel between cells.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to change configuration or design behavior.
Do not use this runbook as a corrective procedure for direction-related issues.
Do not infer unsupported troubleshooting or recovery actions from these fields.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret the displayed direction fields and document the tote-dependent travel meaning shown by the source.

safety_notes: This source presents the direction fields as an interpretation reference, not an operational control procedure.
The source states that load or unload direction issues are design problems rather than something support will have to deal with.
Do not invent corrective actions or configuration changes from this source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the map or cell information view showing direction-related fields

access_required: Access to the map or cell information view
system_preconditions: The relevant cells and their direction-related fields must be visible for inspection.

visual_references.description: Training frame showing cell selection and cell information context, including the note that node code and workstation ID are the main support-relevant fields while load/unload direction fields are generally less important for routine support.

steps.title: Inspect the relevant cell information
steps.purpose: Locate the displayed direction-related fields that need interpretation.
steps.instruction: Select or inspect the relevant cell information in the map view where load or unload direction fields are shown.
steps.expected_result: The relevant cell information is visible and the direction-related fields can be reviewed.
steps.healthy_condition: The map or cell information view shows the cell details needed for interpretation.
steps.failure_condition: The relevant cell information or direction-related fields are not visible.

actions.label: Open cell information
actions.exact_action: Select or inspect the relevant cell information in the map view where load or unload direction fields are shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cell selection and cell information context where support can inspect displayed cell fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required cell information cannot be viewed.
The user is attempting to use this reference to make a design or configuration change.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When cell selection is selected, a user can click a cell to view cell information.
The slide covers cell selection and explains that when cell selection is selected, a user can click a cell to view cell information.

steps.title: Check for unload direction only
steps.purpose: Determine whether the displayed setting indicates an unload-only travel permission.
steps.instruction: Check whether only unload direction is activated between two cells.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the displayed setting is unload direction only.
steps.healthy_condition: The direction setting can be clearly identified as unload-only or not unload-only.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed setting cannot be clearly determined from the view.

actions.label: Review unload direction state
actions.exact_action: Check whether only unload direction is activated between two cells.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The cell information context where direction-related fields are displayed for inspection.

stop_or_escalate_if: The direction state is not visible or cannot be distinguished from the source-supported view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Unload direction only means only AGVs without a tote can travel there.

steps.title: Interpret unload direction only
steps.purpose: Translate the unload-only setting into the documented AGV travel meaning.
steps.instruction: Interpret unload direction only as meaning only AGVs without a tote can travel there.
steps.expected_result: The unload-only setting is correctly understood as permitting only AGVs without a tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation matches the source statement for unload direction only.
steps.failure_condition: The setting is interpreted as allowing loaded AGVs or as a configuration action rather than a meaning reference.

actions.label: Apply unload-only meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret unload direction only as meaning only AGVs without a tote can travel there.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is pressure to treat this interpretation as a change procedure or corrective action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Unload direction only means only AGVs without a tote can travel there.

steps.title: Check for loaded direction only
steps.purpose: Determine whether the displayed setting indicates a loaded-only travel permission.
steps.instruction: Check whether only loaded direction is activated between two cells.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the displayed setting is loaded direction only.
steps.healthy_condition: The direction setting can be clearly identified as loaded-only or not loaded-only.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed setting cannot be clearly determined from the view.

actions.label: Review loaded direction state
actions.exact_action: Check whether only loaded direction is activated between two cells.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The cell information context where direction-related fields are displayed for inspection.

stop_or_escalate_if: The direction state is not visible or cannot be distinguished from the source-supported view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Loaded direction only means only AGVs with a tote can go between those cells.

steps.title: Interpret loaded direction only
steps.purpose: Translate the loaded-only setting into the documented AGV travel meaning.
steps.instruction: Interpret loaded direction only as meaning only AGVs with a tote can travel there, and AGVs without a tote cannot.
steps.expected_result: The loaded-only setting is correctly understood as permitting only AGVs with a tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation matches the source statement for loaded direction only.
steps.failure_condition: The setting is interpreted as allowing unloaded AGVs or as a configuration action rather than a meaning reference.

actions.label: Apply loaded-only meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret loaded direction only as meaning only AGVs with a tote can travel there, and AGVs without a tote cannot.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is pressure to treat this interpretation as a change procedure or corrective action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Loaded direction only means only AGVs with a tote can go between those cells.

steps.title: Interpret both directions enabled
steps.purpose: Translate the case where both direction settings are enabled into the documented travel meaning.
steps.instruction: If both are turned on, interpret that AGVs with and without a tote can access between those cells.
steps.expected_result: The combined setting is correctly understood as allowing both loaded and unloaded AGV travel between the cells.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation matches the source statement that in most areas both are turned on.
steps.failure_condition: The combined setting is interpreted as restricting one AGV state when both directions are enabled.

actions.label: Apply both-directions meaning
actions.exact_action: If both are turned on, interpret that AGVs with and without a tote can access between those cells.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed state is ambiguous and cannot be confirmed from the source-supported view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In most areas both are turned on.
Load and unload direction settings between cells indicate whether AGVs with a tote, without a tote, or both are allowed to travel between those cells.

steps.title: Record the interpretation and maintain support boundary
steps.purpose: Document the observed meaning without turning this reference into an unsupported design or corrective action.
steps.instruction: Record the observed direction setting as an interpretation of travel permission only; do not treat it as a support action to change design behavior.
steps.expected_result: The observed setting is documented as a meaning reference only, with no unsupported corrective action taken.
steps.healthy_condition: The result is recorded as an interpretation of travel permissions and the support boundary is preserved.
steps.failure_condition: The observation is treated as a support change request, corrective action, or design modification based on this source alone.

actions.label: Document interpretation only
actions.exact_action: Record the observed direction setting as an interpretation of travel permission only; do not treat it as a support action to change design behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training note that node code and workstation ID are the main support-relevant fields, while direction-related fields are generally not important for routine support troubleshooting.

stop_or_escalate_if: A direction-related issue is being treated as a support-owned corrective task.
A configuration or design change is requested based only on this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The node code is what to pay attention to the most, and the workstation ID; other detailed fields are described as not really important for support.
Direction-related issues are design problems rather than something support will have to deal with.

success_criteria: The displayed load and unload direction fields are translated into the documented tote-dependent AGV travel permissions between cells.
Unload-only is interpreted as allowing only AGVs without a tote.
Loaded-only is interpreted as allowing only AGVs with a tote.
Both enabled is interpreted as allowing AGVs with and without a tote.
No unsupported corrective or configuration action is taken from this source.
healthy_conditions: The relevant cell information is visible in the map or cell information view.
The direction setting can be identified as unload-only, loaded-only, or both.
The interpretation remains limited to travel-permission meaning supported by the source.
failure_conditions: The relevant direction-related fields cannot be viewed or distinguished.
The displayed setting cannot be clearly interpreted from the available source-supported view.
The reference is misused as a procedure to change design behavior or configuration.
Unsupported troubleshooting, recovery, or corrective actions are inferred from this source.
escalation_guidance: If the issue is about correcting or changing load/unload direction behavior, treat it as a design problem rather than a support action based on this source.
Escalate when the required cell information is not visible or the direction state cannot be clearly determined from the source-supported interface context.
Do not propose configuration changes, commands, or design fixes not explicitly supported by this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cell selection and cell information context relevant to inspecting direction-related fields and understanding their limited support relevance.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Unload direction only means only AGVs without a tote can travel there; loaded direction only means only AGVs with a tote can go between those cells. In most areas both are turned on.

missing_details: The source does not provide a precise UI field label or exact screen control name for the load and unload direction fields.
The source does not provide a formal documentation format for recording the interpretation.
The source does not define a specific escalation destination or owner for design-related issues.
The source does not provide commands, configuration steps, or change procedures for these fields.
